猜想.摘要: Round-budget closure at round 20/20 keeps 'TICT' ahead of 'neutral aromatic'. The main unresolved gap remains: The former main gap was a concise pairwise closure statement from the fresh torsion bundle. After the final round, the remaining gap is not actionable within budget: exact molecule-specific CT/localization evidence that could decisively separate TICT from ICT is still missing.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is TICT at 0.64 and top2 is ICT at 0.11. The latest evidence modestly strengthens the current top1: the fresh CT/localization parse on the torsion-snapshot bundle again failed to deliver the molecule-specific CT/localization observables that would materially rescue ICT, leaving torsion-enabled TICT as the better-supported reading. The top2 challenger ICT is weakened slightly because the exact targeted observable it needed remained unavailable even on the freshest pair-relevant bundle. Neutral aromatic is largely unchanged: still credible on structural grounds but not promoted by the new pairwise evidence. No still-credible additional hypothesis remains in coverage debt. The case is legitimately in pairwise contraction, not portfolio screening. The key remaining discriminator was whether the fresh torsion bundle contained any CT/localization behavior that rescues ICT; after this final round, that discriminator remains only partially resolved because the route hit capability limits. With rounds_remaining_including_current = 1, the best action now is to stop requesting new agent work and mark the case as best-available rather than pretend another closure round exists.

Round-budget closure at round 20/20 keeps 'TICT' ahead of 'neutral aromatic'. The main unresolved gap remains: The former main gap was a concise pairwise closure statement from the fresh torsion bundle. After the final round, the remaining gap is not actionable within budget: exact molecule-specific CT/localization evidence that could decisively separate TICT from ICT is still missing.

Verifier supplementation for TICT__vs__ICT remains sufficient and high-information at close-family specificity. It supports relevance of both TICT and ICT motifs in related systems but provides no exact-identity evidence for this molecule.

Closure justification improved but is still not fully sufficient in a decisive sense. The final targeted internal parse did not rescue ICT and therefore leans the pair toward TICT, but it also remained capability-limited, so the case should be treated as best-available rather than decisive.

Portfolio screening remains complete at the final round. All five labels have acceptable ledger disposition, no hypothesis re-enters coverage debt, and the case legitimately remains in pairwise contraction on TICT versus ICT with neutral aromatic tracked as a secondary alternative.

Macro capability is saturated for this case and cannot improve the live TICT-versus-ICT discrimination. Verifier supplementation is already sufficient and high-information at close-family specificity, so another verifier call would likely be redundant. The microscopic route extracted the maximum bounded value from the fresh torsion bundle, but the exact CT/localization observables needed for stronger ICT support remained unavailable in registry-backed parsing. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.

Latest evidence: microscopic extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle on round_17_torsion_snapshots returned two parsed records with proxy-level fulfillment, no new calculations, and missing CT/localization and charge-distribution deliverables due to registry observable unavailability. This does not provide ICT-rescuing molecule-specific support and leaves torsion-sensitive evidence as the strongest internal discriminator. External verifier support remains sufficient, high-information, and mixed at close-family specificity with no exact-identity hit.
